#転送 [[久津川車塚古墳#丸塚古墳]]
{{Coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=title|name=丸塚古墳}} {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: [[久津川車塚古墳]]から分割（自身の執筆分）。
revision.text:
== 丸塚古墳 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳
（久津川丸塚古墳） |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」 |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、車塚古墳の東方約150メートルに位置する古墳{{Sfn|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。車塚古墳と合わせて国の史跡に指定されている。 === 規模 === 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・形象埴輪（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は、未調査のため明らかでない。 この丸塚古墳は、久津川車塚古墳と同時期の5世紀前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料|2015年}}。 === 規模 === 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル {{Coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=title|name=丸塚古墳}} {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳
（久津川丸塚古墳） |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」 |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 規模 == 城陽市北部、[[木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む、古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は、未調査のため明らかでない。 この丸塚古墳は、久津川車塚古墳と同時期の5世紀前半頃の築造と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 == 規模 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（いずれも城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡完全ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: 国の史跡に係る事柄について加筆。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳
（久津川丸塚古墳） |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 規模 == 城陽市北部、[[木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む、古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は、未調査のため明らかでない。 この丸塚古墳は、久津川車塚古墳と同時期の5世紀前半頃の築造と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 規模 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち） *: 昭和54年1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}}。 *: 平成28年10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に、「[[芭蕉塚古墳]]」・「久世小学校古墳」の2基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（いずれも城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡完全ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 規模 == 城陽市北部、[[木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む、古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は、未調査のため明らかでない。 この丸塚古墳は、久津川車塚古墳と同時期の5世紀前半頃の築造と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 規模 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち） *: 昭和54年1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}}。 *: 平成28年10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に、「[[芭蕉塚古墳]]」・「久世小学校古墳」の2基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡完全ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 規模 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち） *: 昭和54年1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}}。 *: 平成28年10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に、「[[芭蕉塚古墳]]」・「[[久世小学校古墳]]」の2基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「[[久津川古墳群]]」に変更{{PDFlink|[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 五里ごり館（城陽市歴史民俗資料館、城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川車塚・丸塚古墳}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

墳丘 |別名 = 久津川丸塚古墳 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:久津川古墳群 縮尺模型.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[久津川古墳群]] 縮尺模型}}{{small|[[芝ヶ原古墳|史跡芝ヶ原古墳公園]]展示。古墳群を西方から望む。}}]] 城陽市北部、[[木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む、古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は、未調査のため明らかでない。 この丸塚古墳は、久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 規模 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 五里ごり館（城陽市歴史民俗資料館、城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

墳丘 |別名 = 久津川丸塚古墳 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 城陽市北部、[[木津川 (京都府)|木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。主体部の埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。以上より、築造時期は久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 構造 == 丸塚古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 五里ごり館（城陽市歴史民俗資料館、城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ9.6m |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 城陽市北部、[[木津川 (京都府)|木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部が2段築成で、前方部が1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。以上より、築造時期は久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 五里ごり館（城陽市歴史民俗資料館、城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連項目 == * [[久津川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} * [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/introduction/asset/kuni-siseki/kuni-siseki2 史跡 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市ホームページ * [http://xn--6oqz6c35b6zh48ipn2e0ys.jp/html/kankou/rekisi3.html#33 国指定の史跡・重要文化財 > 久津川車塚・丸塚古墳] - 城陽市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ9.6m |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）展示。}}]] 城陽市北部、[[木津川 (京都府)|木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部では2段築成、前方部では1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が検出されており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。築造時期は久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）（城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 丸塚古墳説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|久津川古墳群（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|久津川車塚-丸塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{Ancient Japanese Burial Mounds |Name = Maruzuka Kofun |Image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |Location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place Name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |Location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place Name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - {{Infobox ancient sites |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} were designated as "Kutsukawa Kurazuka and Maruzuka Kofun" as a Historic ... 6 KB (708 words) - 01:11, 15 January [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kofun stubs]] 2025 Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka ...shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizugawa River|ja|木津川 (京都府)}}. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] Kutsukawa Kofun Group ...a group of ancient burial mounds ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan.
