{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Burial Mounds" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Burial Mounds|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Burial Mounds}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Kofuns") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The burial mound is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 burial mounds, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole burial mound with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Kofuns" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Kofuns|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. (Part of "Kuzugawa Kofuns") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Kofuns" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Kofuns|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Kofuns") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Kofuns" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Kofuns|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Kofuns") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Kofuns" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Kofuns|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} Mound |Alias = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun |Group = {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial Facility = Unknown |Buried Person = |Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historical Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kuzugawa Kofuns") |Notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that form the {{ill|Kuzugawa Kofuns|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Information Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. Artifacts such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its shape, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kuzugawa Kofuns") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kuzugawa Kofuns" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Information Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kuzugawa Kofuns|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Database|401|1741|Kuzugawa Kofuns}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuzugawa Kofuns]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan.
