Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_other_designation = National other_other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō (Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of Cultural Property Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 Designation"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. (Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |other_designation = National other_designation: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National other_designation on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National other_designation Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National other_designation)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) -
