Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}}

The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}}

Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. (Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. (Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Height: 9.6m |Construction = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}{{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Group") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" after the addition of {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=draft}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Group|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=draft}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=draft}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Bashōzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jōyō City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] [[ja:%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%9F%8E%E9%99%BD%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:aruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)]] {{citation needed|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 丸塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = 久津川丸塚古墳 |所属 = [[久津川古墳群]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=久津川丸塚古墳}} |所在地 = [[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m
高さ9.6m |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）展示。}}]] 城陽市北部、[[木津川 (京都府)|木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部では2段築成、前方部では1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が認められており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。築造時期は久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）（城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=京都府の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]26|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|京都府の地名|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|国指定史跡ガイド}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ9.6m |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「丸塚古墳」
（「久津川古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = }} '''丸塚古墳'''（まるづかこふん、久津川丸塚古墳）は、[[京都府]][[城陽市]]平川車塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[久津川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「久津川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）展示。}}]] 城陽市北部、[[木津川 (京都府)|木津川]]右岸の丘陵地に築造された古墳である。丘陵一帯では、本古墳や[[久津川車塚古墳]]・[[芭蕉塚古墳]]の大型古墳3基を含む古墳100基以上からなる[[久津川古墳群]]の分布が知られる。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形丸塚古墳説明板。。墳丘は後円部では2段築成、前方部では1段築成。墳丘長は80メートル。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・蓋形・甲冑形埴輪）が認められており、中でも家形埴輪（5棟以上）のうち1棟は、高さ約1メートルに及ぶ大型のものである。墳丘周囲には墳丘と相似形の周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた全長は104メートルに及ぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。築造時期は久津川車塚古墳と同時期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf 「2015年度発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（城陽市教育委員会、2015年） - リンクは有限会社京都平安文化財。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 古墳総長：104メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：80メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：63メートル ** 高さ：東側で8.3メートル、西側で9.6メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：32メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：26.5メートル == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 丸塚古墳（史跡「[[久津川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）1月19日に「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）10月3日、既指定の史跡「久津川車塚・丸塚古墳」に[[芭蕉塚古墳]]・[[久世小学校古墳]]の古墳2基を追加指定して、指定名称を「久津川古墳群」に変更{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf 「史跡等の指定等について」]}}（文化庁記者発表、2016年6月17日）。平成28年10月3日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 関連施設 == * 城陽市歴史民俗資料館（五里ごり館）（城陽市寺田今堀） - 丸塚古墳の出土埴輪を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（城陽市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=久津川古墳群|title=京都府の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]26|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|京都府の地名|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B9%85%E6%B4%A5%E5%B7%9D%E8%BB%8A%E5%A1%9A-%E4%B8%B8%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443816 久津川車塚-丸塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|国指定史跡ガイド}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1741|久津川古墳群}} {{デフォルトソート:まるつかこふん しようようし}} [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]] Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes = }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the [[:en:Kizu_River|Kizu River]]. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|qq=}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|qq=}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City, Japan.
