Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|qq=}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|version=104621342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28608911}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28608911]] Wikidata name: Maruzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes = |coordinates = {{Coord|34.865189|N|135.781733|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu_River|en|Kizu_River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426315}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|qq=}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|version=104621342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28608911}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes = |coordinates = {{Coord|34.865189|N|135.781733|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu_River|en|Kizu_River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426315}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|qq=}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|version=104621342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28608911}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes = |coordinates = {{Coord|34.865189|N|135.781733|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu_River|en|Kizu_River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426315}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|qq=}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|version=104621342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28608911}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426315}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu_River|en|Kizu_River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. [[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)]][[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maruzuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo), funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{coord|34|51|54.68|N|135|46|54.24|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}} Hirakawa Kurumazuka |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 80m
Height: 9.6m |built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |burial = Unknown |Buried Person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Historic Site = National Historic Site: "Maruzuka Kofun"
(Part of "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes = |coordinates = {{Coord|34.865189|N|135.781733|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }} '''Maruzuka Kofun''' (丸塚古墳, also known as Kuzugawa Maruzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the mounds that type the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster." == Overview == [[File:丸塚古墳 (城陽市) 出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan).}}]] The Kofun is located in the northern part of Jōyō City, on a hill along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu_River|en|Kizu_River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large mounds such as this one, {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, are distributed throughout the area. The mound is a scallop-shaped keyhole Kofun with a short anterior projectionMaruzuka Kofun Intypeation Board.. The posterior circular section is built in two tiers, while the anterior section is built in one tier. The mound measures 80 meters in length. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, lotus-shaped Haniwa, and {{ill|Figurative Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative Haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (house-shaped, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped) have been recovered from the mound's surface. Among the house-shaped Haniwa, one measures approximately 1 meter in height, making it a significant artifact. Surrounding the mound is a moat that mirrors its type, extending the overall length to 104 meters. The burial facility has not been investigated. The construction period is estimated to be around the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, coinciding with {{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369464}}{{PDFlink|[http://iseki-hakktsu.com/investigation/kustukawakurumazuka20150919.pdf "2015 Field Excavation Report"]}} (Jōyō City Board of Education, 2015).. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979. == Mound Specifications == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 104 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 80 meters * Posterior section (2-tiered construction): ** Diameter: 63 meters ** Height: 8.3 meters on the east side, 9.6 meters on the west side * Anterior section (1-tiered construction): ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 32 meters ** Height: approximately 2 meters * Neck section: ** Width: 26.5 meters == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Maruzuka Kofun (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") *: Designated as a National Historic Site on January 19, 1979, under the name "Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun"{{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}}. *: On October 3, 2016, the name was changed to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426315}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf "Notice of designation1 designation1"]}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).MEXT Notification No. 144, October 3, 2016.. == Related Facilities == * Jōyō City Historical Folklore Museum (Gorikkan) (Terada Imahorii, Jōyō City) - Stores and displays Haniwa excavated from Maruzuka Kofun. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeation Board (Jōyō City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Kyoto City Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1979|chapter=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harv|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=National Historic Site Guide: Kuzugawa Kurumazuka and Maruzuka Kofun|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha|1=Kodansha}}|ref={{Harv|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka-Maruzuka Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} * {{Historic Site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} * [[Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City, Japan. Parts of the group have been des ...tsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} * [[Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Kyo City)|qq=}} * {{nihongo|[[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]}} * {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:芭蕉塚古墳|芭蕉塚古墳]]}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|version=104621342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Maruzuka Kofun (Joyo)}} [[ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28608911}} [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
